375:
Finish Trim Interior - Doors, Hardware, Trim Options  
Scope of Work: Contractor shall provide and install specified finish trim packages, doors, and hardware options for interior applications.  
Included Feature: Interior Door & Trim Package includes 2-Panel Square, Raised doors, 9/16” x 5-1/4” Coronado Baseboard Model MDF223A, and 5/8” x 2-1/4” Colonial Door Casing Model MDF239A.  
Optional Upgrade: Interior Door & Trim Package 1 comprises 2-Panel Square, Recessed doors, 1/2” x 5-1/2” Round Edge Baseboard Model MDF206A, and 9/16” x 3-1/4” Round Edge Door Casing Model MDF234A.  
Included Feature: Hardware includes Schlage Stratus Knob, Chrome with matching hinges.  
Optional Upgrade: Upgrade to Schlage Broadway Lever with Century Backplate, available in multiple finishes.  
Optional Upgrade: Schlage Connect Smart Deadbolt Z-Wave with illuminated touchscreen control and built-in alarm sensors.  
Optional Upgrade: Interior Door at Primary Bath for added privacy.  
Optional Upgrade: Primary Bath Interior Barn Door with hardware finish choices of Black or Stainless.  
Included Feature: Pantry Door in Residence 3, consistent with interior door selection.  
Optional Upgrade: 1-lite Frosted Glass door option for Pantry Door.  
Optional Upgrade: Mirrored Closet Doors in various configurations for Residences 1, 2, and 3, sold per room.  
Note: All features and upgrades are subject to availability and are preliminary, not contracted.
371:
Windows & Installation SOW - Reserve  
Contractor shall supply and install windows, sliding glass doors, and exterior French doors, adhering to specific performance and energy standards.  
Windows and Doors: All windows and sliding glass doors must meet Title 24 energy performance ratings, including Low E and dual glazing.  
Acoustic Requirements: STC rating of 26; additional requirements per architectural design and acoustical report if applicable.  
U-Values: All products to comply with NFRC standards and meet minimum U-Value per Title 24.  
Labeling: Each window and door unit must have a label indicating U-Value, SHGC, and STC ratings.  
Installation Conditions: Confirm opening sizes, squareness, and levels; report discrepancies to Builder immediately.  
Security Features: All installations must meet local security ordinances.  
Delivery: Deliver windows in complete packages per house, by 9:00 AM on scheduled dates, marked with lot numbers.  
Material Protection: Adequate packing is required to safeguard materials from environmental exposure; damaged units must be reported.  
Warranty: Glazing, frames, and moving parts are warranted against defects per manufacturer’s warranty for SB800.  
Installation Certificate: Provide completed Form CF-6R for energy compliance for each lot.  
Flashing: Utilize Rainbuster 420 for flashing installation, extending past king studs and header beams.  
Sealant Application: Use Rainbuster 450 polyurethane sealant for window installations, applied in specified bead sizes.  
Fastening Requirements: Attach windows with corrosion-resistant screws, perimeter fastened every 10”, avoiding corners.  
Cleaning: Excess sealant must be cleaned using manufacturer-approved materials as work progresses.
367:
CLNCONS – Construction Rough Clean SOW  
Contractor shall furnish all labor, materials, and equipment necessary for rough clean construction services at Reserve at Capital, Fremont, CA.

Trade Contractor Responsibilities:  Includes all labor, material, equipment, taxes, supervision, and delivery for rough clean inside homes and garages.  
Recycling Compliance: Must adhere to recycling requirements and avoid placing toxic materials, concrete, oil, or cleaning rags in dumpsters.  
Damage Responsibility: Responsible for damage to improvements or landscaping when accessing rear yards. Do not drive across sidewalks; park only in approved areas.  

Stage 1 Requirements:  
Staffing: Provide staff every Friday for weekend clean-up; work early mornings for marketing.  
Debris Removal: Remove debris from homes as needed, using on-site dumpsters.  
Interior Cleaning: Clean homes and garages post-roof sheathing and roof operations; ensure sweep prior to all Framing Inspections (minimum two sweeps).  
Exterior Cleaning: Pickup debris, lathing wire, and tar paper around houses before setting scaffolds.  
Pre-Insulation Cleaning: Sweep and clean interior homes before insulation work.

Stage 2 Requirements:  
Painting Preparation: Sweep for prime coat painting post-finish trim and cabinet installation. Clean FAU platform and cold air return; coordinate weekly with the Superintendent.  

Stage 3 Requirements:  
Lot Cleanup: Remove all debris before fine grading operations.  
Final Yard Cleanup: Clean yards, rake and remove trash after lot fencing installation; do not place concrete debris in dumpsters.  

QC Clean Walk:  
Interior Cleaning: Sweep and scrape floors for carpet and tile layers; pressure wash as part of the final clean.  

Additional Cleans:  
Authorization Required: Perform additional cleaning tasks as authorized, including interior sweeps and exterior debris removal.  

Project Specifications Date: 06/30/2026  
Reference: Additional requirements in Exhibit C Contract Specifications.
366:
Joint Trench, Service Completions - Scope of Work  
Contractor shall perform trenching, excavation, and associated work for the installation of Joint Trench and Service Completions.

Cost Code: PLUMUG/ELECUG  
Scope: Trenching, excavations, substructure installation on a per unit cost basis.  
Utility Standards: All work must adhere to city Electric and Gas Department, PG&E, AT&T, Comcast, and related utility standards.  
Labor and Equipment: Contractor to provide all labor, materials, and equipment to meet project schedule.  
Conduit Requirements: Supply and labor to prove existing 2”, 4”, and 6” conduits, including mandrel and pull rope.  
Safety Coordination: PG&E representative must be contacted for safety clearances prior to conduit proving.  
Traffic Control: Normal traffic control is required during the work.  
Soil Management: Excess soil must be spread on site or removed per Project Superintendent's direction.  
Permits: Contractor is responsible for all permit expenses.  
Utility Identification: Contractor to coordinate with Underground Service Alert (USA) prior to any excavation.  
Damage Responsibility: Contractor must repair or replace any damaged facilities at no additional cost.  
Safety Compliance: Familiarity with all Cal OSHA, city, and PG&E safety requirements is mandatory.  
Inspection: All work requires inspection by the PG&E representative.  
Electrical Completions: Includes wire, splicing, secondary box, and termination at meter panel.  
Exclusions: Meter panel and foundation riser not included in completion scope.  
Trenching and Backfilling: Includes trench, backfilling, and utility coordination.  
Building Move-In: 1 building per move-in for model building; production is 2 buildings per move-in unless otherwise arranged.  
Refer to: Exhibit C for additional contract specifications.
359:
ELECTRIC - Electrical SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work: Contractor shall provide electrical wiring installation and supervision in accordance with current codes and specifications.

General Requirements:  
1. Compliance: All work must comply with current N.E.C. and local building codes.  
2. Modifications: Changes in specifications require prior written approval from Builder.  
3. Documentation: Provide red-line As-Built drawings and load calculations prior to setting panels.  
4. Testing: Conduct necessary tests to verify proper installation; all circuits must be operable before final acceptance.  
5. Infrastructure: Supply structural metal straps, nail plates, underground service sleeves, and utility housings as required.  
6. Temporary Power: Install 4” steel riser with temporary power panel and 50amp breaker for both Single Family and Multi-Family setups.  
7. Sweeps: All service sweeps must be at a minimum depth of 24”, with specific radius and diameter requirements for utilities.  
8. Materials: Use corrosion-resistant screws, U.L. listed materials, and Title 24 compliant fixtures.  
9. Electrical Wiring: Install all 12-2g and 14-2g U.L. listed non-metallic sheathed cable, circuit breakers, and receptacles as per code.  
10. Detectors: Install U.L. listed smoke detectors, carbon monoxide detectors, and required low voltage wiring.  
11. Lighting: Adhere to Title 24 high efficacy requirements for lighting in kitchens and toilet areas.  

Coordination and Cleanup:  
1. Collaboration: Coordinate with Plumbing, Framing, Fire Sprinkler, and HVAC contractors for maximum efficiency.  
2. Cleanup: Remove all debris from cutting and drilling operations as required.  
3. Punch List: Complete all punch list items within the schedule provided post-homeowner walkthrough.
358:
Drywall - Drywall SOW  
Contractor shall furnish and install drywall at the Reserve at Capital, Fremont, CA.  
Material Specifications: All wallboard must be from USG, National, American, Temple, CertainTeed, LaFarge North America, Pabco, or GP.  
Gypsum Board: Single layer 5/8” Type X at firewalls and garage; 5/8” for ceilings; ½” tapered drywall for living areas.  
Dens-Shield: Required at all tub and shower walls; must extend to ceiling and 12 inches past edges, or 24 inches above deck for bathtubs.  
Installation Standards: Adhere to all manufacturer guidelines; inspect framing for plumbness; notify of any issues 2 days prior to installation.  
Stacking: Gypsum must be stacked perpendicularly on 2x4 stickers and 12” away from walls; covered with heavy plastic at all times.  
Joint Compound: U.S.G. or equal for all joint compounds; three applications of joint compound required at seams with 12” minimum tape width.  
Texture: Uniform knock down texture to cover seams; any excess texture thicker than models must be removed.  
Corners: All angles and corners to be finished, including intersections at vaulted ceilings and skylights.  
Inspections: Notify Project Superintendent for any missing insulation; utilize existing openings for material receiving and protect frames.  
Repairs: Contractor must repair any damages caused during installations or reassembly of doors and windows.

328:
Insulation and Installation  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall supply and install foam, batt, and blown insulation in accordance with Title 24 requirements.  
Insulation Types:  Foam, batt, and blown insulation shall be used; formaldehyde-treated insulation material is prohibited.  
Phased Installation:  Insulation shall be installed in the following phases: Pre-Insulation, Polycel after Rough Electrical, Batt Insulation, and Blown Insulation at flat areas.  
Installation Quality:  Insulation must avoid compression around electrical boxes and shall be fluffed to full potential.  
Ventilation:  No insulation shall block floor, roof, or wall vents; unfaced insulation must fit tight to the cavity.  
Special Areas:  Sloped and flat attic ceilings, crawl spaces, and cantilever sections must be insulated according to T24 requirements.  
Fire Sprinkler Insulation:  Insulation above fire sprinkler pipes in attic space is required as per local jurisdiction.  
Securing Insulation:  All batt insulation under conditioned spaces must touch subfloor sheathing above and create an air gap below.  
Pre-Insulation:  Install pre-insulation material in inaccessible areas including fireplace chases, rim joists, and behind tubs.  
Sealants:  Polycel foam caulking at air infiltration areas; caulking sealant is required around warm wall plates and sills.  
Firestop Requirements:  Firestop mineral wool and draft stops included where required; fire-rated caulking needed as per code.  
Certification:  Provide three copies of insulation certification noting R-value, thickness, density, and type for each area insulated.  
Insulation Compliance:  Submit CF-6R Insulation Certificates timely to avoid scheduling delays, ensuring registration with CalCERTS if required.  
Additional Specifications:  Refer to Exhibit C Contract Specifications for further requirements.
319:
Fire Alarm - Scope of Work  
Contractor shall furnish, install, test, and certify a complete fire alarm system per City and Fire Department requirements.  
Trade Specific Conditions:  
1. Compliance: Work must conform to current NFPA 72 and all equipment manufacturer's recommendations.  
2. System Components: Include control panel, silent expansion module, mini horns, water flow switch tie-in, PIV tie-in, smoke detectors, pull stations, and exterior bells for each building.  
3. Responsibility: Contractor must adhere to all requirements set by the City and Fire Department; no additional costs for overlooked items will be incurred.  
4. Approval: Design and submit fire alarm system for approval, including design and plan check fees.  
5. Material Standards: All materials must be listed by California State Fire Marshall and UL, with exterior equipment water-tight and properly sealed.  
6. Inspection: Visual inspections of devices required; each control function to be physically activated to confirm proper installation.  
7. Testing: Perform all tests and inspections mandated by City’s Fire Department.  
8. Structural Penetrations: Require prior approval from Structural Engineer; no alterations without written consent.  
9. Coordination: Coordinate all electrical components installation with electrical and fire sprinkler contractors.  
10. Fire/Sound Assembly Compliance: Follow all plans, acoustical report, and local codes for isolation pads and vibration isolators.  
11. Fire Stopping: Fire blocks, stops, and caulking must be installed as required, using approved Metacaulk 1000 Firestopping Sealant only.  
12. Layout & Installation: Include layout, installation, core drilling and fire-stopping of penetrations through fire-rated and acoustic-rated walls.  
13. Project Specifications Date: 06/29/2026; refer to Exhibit C for additional requirements.
313:
Garage Doors SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install sectional garage doors and openers according to specifications.  
Craft Code: GRGDOORS – Garage Doors; GRGDROPR – Garage Door Openers  
Metal Thickness: All metal doors shall be a minimum of 26 gauge.  
Panel Thickness: Minimum of 2” for all doors.  
Spring Specifications: All doors shall be equipped with torsion type, oil tempered, stress relieved springs with a cycle minimum of 15,000.  
Track Hardware: Standard track hardware with torsion springs shall be provided.  
Weather Seal: A synthetic rubber weather seal shall be installed at the bottom of each door.  
Drums: 16' doors shall have 4" tapered drums for easy operation.  
Hinges: Heavy duty hinges shall be 14 gauge.  
Opener Installation: Hard-wired, wall-mounted opener controllers shall be installed flush, level, and plumb, with concealed wiring.  
Safety Compliance: Reversing safety system garage door operator shall meet or exceed Federal Law UL 325.  
Emergency Release: Garage doors must include an emergency release if no other entry access exists.  
Service: Installation adjustments shall include replacing damaged doors due to manufacturing and installation defects.  
Painting: If painted, Tradecontractor shall verify specified paint and paint LRV meet manufacturer’s requirements.  
Refer to Exhibit C: Additional requirements located in Exhibit C Contract Specifications.
311:
Excavation Final Grade SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall perform excavation final grading work per plot plans, including rough and finish grading for lots and installation of erosion control fabric.  
Cost Code: EXCFGRD  
Rough Grade: Grade lots to plus or minus 0.1' for drainage; re-establish swales and flow lines.  
Slope Percentages: Establish at right angles to perimeter walls for a distance of not less than 3 feet.  
Utility Boxes: Re-expose all utility boxes.  
Mirafi Fabric: Supply and install with 4" coil pins; install 7.5' wide in side yards and around foundation perimeter.  
Finish Grade: Grade for fence lines; ensure no excessive rocks or dirt clods remain.  
Spoils: All spoils to be lost over adjacent lots at direction of project superintendent.  
Access: Areas inaccessible to equipment require manual labor.  
Final Sweeping: Sweep flatwork and driveways post-grading.  
Street Cleaning: Scrape streets to remove soil deposits from grading operations.  
Damages: Contractor is responsible for damages caused by equipment, including concrete, sidewalks, and walls.  
Backfill: Complete backfill of any utility trenches with minor soil settlement.  
Inspection: Inspect each lot for damages prior to commencing work.  
City Approval: All grading work must be approved by the City, civil engineers, and Contractor.  
Project Specifications Date: 07/13/2026

299:
HVAC - HVAC Sheetmetal SOW  
Contractor shall furnish and install heating, ventilation, air conditioning systems, and associated sheet metal components for the Reserve at Capital project in Fremont, CA.  
Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning: System must deliver conditioned air to each room with complaints directed to Tradecontractor.  
Ducts: Minimum R-6 insulation required; ducts must be sealed per ACM Manual; must conform to N.F.P.A. 90A and 90B.  
Thermostat: Programmable, conforms to Title 24, includes low voltage wiring.  
Installation Certificate: Complete Form CF-1R for each lot to ensure compliance with energy calculations.  
Airflow: Must match ACCA Manual J calculations within +/- 10%; pressure balanced not to exceed +/- 3 pascals.  
Condenser: Provide polyethylene pads level to grade; comply with energy consultant's SEER ratings.  
Fresh Air Ventilation: Must meet Cal Green and ASHRAE 62.2 requirements.  
Sheet Metal: 26 ga. galvanized and acid etched exterior sheet metal; all flashing to be bonderized and ready for paint.  
Downspouts: Install supports every 5 feet and at every joint; leaders terminate no more than 3 inches above grade.  
Gutters: Must drain completely into the gutter system without overshooting; ensure proper installation per roofing specifications.  
Flashing: Z-bar flashing at siding, doors, and windows; lead flashing for “S” tile roofs as required.  
Ventilation: Only louvered sheet metal gable vents and foundation vents with screens will be provided.  
Fire/Sound Assembly: Compliance with acoustical report and code requirements, including isolation pads and approved acoustical sealant.

267:
Fire Sprinkler System Bid Document  
Scope: Contractor shall provide and install a new automatic wet pipe fire sprinkler system in accordance with local codes and standards.  
Project Information: Location is Fremont, CA; occupancy type R-2; construction type V-B; 3 stories; total area 21,612 SF.  
Applicable Codes: NFPA 13D, California Building Code 2022, California Fire Code 2022, California Mechanical Code 2022.  
System Testing: All new installations to be tested for leakage at 200 PSI water pressure.  
Pipe Insulation: Non-metallic piping in attics to have minimum 6" insulation to prevent temperature exposure.  
Alarm Requirements: Interior alarms must be audible in all bedrooms; additional interior audible water flow alarm is mandatory.  
Sprinkler Head Details: Type is Viking VK494, model Freedom with a 4.9 K-factor, size ½”, 155°F temperature rating; quick response for garage is Viking VK3021, size ½”, 200°F temperature rating.  
Water Supply Data: Static pressure 59 PSI, residual pressure 57 PSI, flow rate 1030 GPM, sourced from Alameda County Water District.  
Hydraulic Design Criteria: Residential system requires 0.10 GPM/SQFT design density; Ordinary hazard groups use 0.15 GPM/SQFT.  
Pipe Bracing: Branch lines must be braced within 6 inches of supports; hanger load bearing surface needs to be at least ½" wide.  
Maintenance Responsibility: Owner must maintain the sprinkler system and replace any damaged sprinkler heads.  
Seismic Bracing: Flexible couplings required on both sides of penetrations; 4-way brace necessary for risers exceeding 3 feet.  
Installation Verification: Contractor to verify conditions, water flow data, and hydraulic calculations before installation.  
Owner's Manual: A laminated manual must be mounted adjacent to the spare head box.  
Combined Domestic/Fire System: Underground piping must be flushed and inspected by the building inspector prior to connection.

Contractor shall perform trenching, excavation, and associated work for joint trench installation and service completions at Grant at Capital, Fremont, CA.  
Cost Code: PLUMUG/ELECUG  

Trenching and Excavation:  Includes trenching, excavations, substructure installation, and will be billed on a per unit cost basis.  
Utility Standards:  Work must comply with All City Electric and Gas Department, PG&E, AT&T, Comcast, and related underground utility standards.  
Labor and Equipment:  Contractor to provide all necessary labor, material, and equipment to meet project schedule dates.

Traffic Control:  Normal traffic control is required during the work.  
Soil Management:  Excess soil to be spread on-site or removed as directed; all excess materials must be removed from the job site after service connection completion.  
Permitting:  Contractor is responsible for obtaining necessary permits.  
Utility Identification:  Contractor must coordinate the identification of underground utilities and contact Underground Service Alert (USA) before starting trenching or excavation.  
Hand Digging:  Hand digging may be necessary but will not incur additional charges.  
Damage Responsibility:  Contractor is liable for any damage to facilities during work and must repair or replace substructures at no additional cost.  
Safety Compliance:  Adherence to Cal OSHA, City, and PG&E safety requirements is mandatory during performance.  
Inspection Requirement:  All work is subject to inspection by a PG&E representative.

2. Electrical Completions:  Includes pipe, location wire, riser, fittings, and stub tie-in (excluding meter, regulators, manifolds, and house plumbing).  
3. Utility Coordination:  Includes trenching, backfilling, and coordination; movement includes one building per move-in, production limited to two buildings unless otherwise arranged.

Scope of Work: Contractor shall install a wet-type automatic fire sprinkler system at a 6-plex residential building in Fremont, CA.  
Occupancy Type: R-2; Construction Type: V-B; Number of Stories: 3; Actual Gross SF: 16,379 SF.  
Design Compliance: System design shall be in accordance with 2022 California Building Code, NFPA 13D, CFC 2022, and applicable NFPA standards.  
Installation Pressure Test: All new system installations shall be tested for leakage at 200 PSI water pressure.  
Piping Insulation: Non-metallic piping in attics requires a minimum of 6" insulation to avoid temperature exposure.  
Alarm Requirements: Interior alarms must be clearly audible in all bedrooms; an interior audible water flow alarm is required.  
Flushing Requirement: Underground piping must be flushed until clear prior to connection to the overhead sprinkler system.  
Pipe Guidelines: Branch lines shall be braced at a distance of six inches or less from tee or elbow drops to the sprinkler head.  
Pipe Hanger Specifications: Must have a load-bearing surface at least ½" wide or provide support in two places.  
Maintenance Responsibility: The owner is responsible for maintaining the automatic fire sprinkler system in operable condition, including replacing painted or damaged sprinkler heads.  
Hydraulic Design Criteria: Residential system: 0.10 GPM/SQFT design density, 100 GPM hose stream demand; Light Hazard: 0.10 GPM/SQFT; Ordinary Hazard Group 1: 0.15 GPM/SQFT.  
Water Supply Data: Source: Alameda County Water District; Static Pressure: 59 PSI; Residual Pressure: 57 PSI; Flow Rate: 1030 GPM.  
Riser Bracing: All riser and cross main piping shall have flexible couplings on both sides of penetrations; a four-way brace is required at the top of all risers exceeding 3 feet in length.

173:
Irrigation Plan Document  
Scope of Work: Contractor shall install the irrigation system per the provided plans and notes, ensuring all components are approved by the owner's authorized representative.

Mainline Location: Actual mainline must be a minimum of 18” away from adjacent hardscape and other obstacles, as indicated for clarity within paving.  
Pipe Sleeves: All piping and wiring must be sleeved under paving, with a minimum diameter of 2x the pipe or wire diameter. Sleeves should extend a minimum of 12 inches beyond paving.  
Valve Location: Electric control valves and isolation valves are approximate; locations must be staked and approved prior to installation. Contractor must accommodate minor modifications without additional cost.  
Nozzle Pattern: Contractor is responsible for adjusting nozzle patterns and radius to achieve 100% coverage while minimizing spray streams on pathways, buildings, and paving.  
Head Layout: Verification of all head layouts with the owner's representative is required before commencing work.  
Controller Assembly: Final controller location to be determined by the owner's representative; electrical connections must conform to local and national codes.  
Point of Connection: Connection shall be a 1-1/2" domestic water meter with a 2" service line installed by others; verification in the field is mandatory.  
Tree Valve: Offsite irrigation package includes tree valve; heads must be located to avoid obstacles within a 3-foot radius.  
Lateral Pipe Size Chart: Sizes and gallonage include: 3/4" (0-5 gpm), 1" (6-10 gpm), 1-1/4" (11-22 gpm), 1-1/2" (23-28 gpm), 2" (29-50 gpm), 2-1/2" (51-70 gpm).  
Coverage Requirement: Contractor to add heads or drip systems to achieve 100% coverage and adjust to vertical obstructions like tree trunks and light poles.  
Communication: Immediate notification is required for discrepancies found in site conditions or plans.  
Familiarization: Contractor must familiarize with site conditions prior to bidding and starting work.
166:
HVAC - HVAC Sheetmetal SOW - Mint  
Contractor shall furnish and install all HVAC equipment, sheet metal components, and ventilation systems as detailed in the scope.  
Heating and Cooling:  Deliver conditioned air per engineered system design; address complaints from homeowners regarding system performance.  
Ducts:  Use wire-flex or sheet metal; all supply ducts to be insulated to a minimum level of R-6; adhere to N.F.P.A. 90A and 90B standards.  
Air Distribution:  Design and install per ACCA Manual J; airflow must match calculations within +/- 10%.  
Registers and Grilles:  Install registers minimum 9" off walls, factory painted white. Return air grilles minimum 3’ from detectors.  
Thermostat:  Provide programmable thermostat conforming to Title 24 requirements, including low voltage wiring.  
Fresh Air Ventilation:  Comply with Cal Green and ASHRAE 62.2 standards.  
Condensate Lines:  Coordinate with plumbing; HVAC contractor responsible for final hook-up at framing inspection.  
Sheet Metal Thickness:  Use minimum 26 ga. galvanized and acid etched metal.  
Flashing:  Provide Z-bar flashing for exterior siding, door, and window installations.  
Gutters:  Install gutters with leaders terminating no greater than 3" above finish grade; no elbows allowed in drainage.  
Vents:  Furnish only louvered sheet metal gable vents; install galvanized vents for mechanical equipment, including water heaters.  
Installation Certificate:  Provide completed Form CF-1R for compliance with energy calculations for each lot.

158:
1. Landscape Design Documentation  
Scope of Work: Contractor shall develop and produce a comprehensive landscape exhibit for the project site including all necessary design elements.

Project Specifications:  
Design Requirements: The landscape exhibit must include a detailed layout of the proposed plantings, hardscaping elements, and other features specific to the project goals.  
Plant Selection: All plant species chosen must be native to the region and suitable for the local climate; a minimum of 20% of the plants must be pollinator-friendly.  
Spacing and Sizing: Shrub spacing must not exceed 3 feet apart; trees shall be a minimum of 2 inches in caliper at time of planting.  
Irrigation: An automated irrigation system must be installed to ensure proper watering of all planted areas; must comply with local water conservation standards.  
Soil Preparation: Topsoil must be assessed and amended as necessary to provide adequate drainage and nutrient levels, with a minimum depth of 12 inches for planting zones.  
Hardscape Materials: Paving stones must meet ASTM C936 specifications; all walkways should have a minimum width of 4 feet and be constructed of permeable materials.  
Fencing: Any fencing included in the landscape must consist of pressure-treated wood or equivalent material, with a height not to exceed 6 feet.  
Maintenance Plan: A maintenance schedule must be provided outlining watering, pruning, and fertilization needs over the first two growing seasons.   
Permits: All necessary permits for work must be obtained prior to commencing with construction; inspections may be required at various stages.  
Buffer Zones: Specific buffer zones around water features must be designated with native grasses to minimize runoff and ensure ecological benefits.

ELECTRIC – Electrical SOW  
Contractor shall provide and install all electrical wiring and components as specified for the Mint at Capital project in Fremont, CA.  
General: Provide supervision, labor, materials, tools, and equipment necessary for electrical work according to N.E.C. and local building codes.  
Compliance: All work must adhere to City, State, and Federal laws; conflicts between documents and law must favor the law.  
Drawings: Submit red-line, As-Built drawings showing circuiting and concealed conduits after Model completion.  
Load Calculations: Provide load calculations prior to panel installation for each plan.  
Testing: Conduct necessary tests to ensure proper installation; all circuits must be operable prior to insulation and final acceptance.  
Temp Power (Single Family): Supply and install a 4” steel electrical riser with panel bars for temporary power, 50amp breaker, and SO cord with female twist lock end.  
Temp Power (Multi-Family): Coordinate switch gear install and provide similar temp power setup requirements.  
Service Sleeves: Install underground service sleeves at 24” depth as per utility company specifications.  
Electrical Wiring: Use U.L. listed materials, including 12-gauge and 14-gauge copper wire for circuits and ensure grounding in line with N.E.C.  
Outlets and Devices: Install U.L. listed receptacles and switches, meeting current Title 24 standards for all outlets.  
Lighting: All recessed can lights must be high efficacy, comply with JA8 certification, and adhere to Title 24 requirements.  
Smoke Detectors: Install U.L. listed interconnected smoke and carbon monoxide detectors with required battery backup.  
Coordination: Consult with Plumbing, Framing, Fire Sprinkler, and HVAC trades to maximize collaboration.  
Punch List: Complete all written punch list items according to the schedule provided by the Project Superintendent.

114:
Drywall - Scope of Work Document  
Contractor shall furnish and install all drywall materials and finishes in accordance with specified requirements.

Manufacturer:  All wallboard shall be from USG, National, American, Temple, CertainTeed, LaFarge North America, Pabco, or GP.  
Material Specifications:  
- Gypsum wall boards to be used.  
- Single layer 5/8” Type X at firewalls, garage ceilings, and garage walls.  
- Interior ceilings to be 5/8”.  
- All other living areas to be ½” tapered drywall.  
- Georgia Pacific Dens-Shield® required at tub and shower walls to ceiling.  
Installation Requirements:  
- Install Dens-Shield behind solid surfaces and extend 12” past edges of tub/shower, or 24” above tub deck where applicable.  
- Two layers of ¼” flex drywall to be used for curved applications.  
- All 'pre-rock' areas to follow Project Superintendent’s schedule.  
- Non-board materials must be U.S.G. or equal, approved in writing by Brookfield Residential.  
Delivery:  
- Gypsum wallboard must be neatly stacked, avoiding sagging or damage.  
- Stacks must be 6” apart and 12” from walls, covered with heavy plastic wrap.  
Board Installation:  
- Adhere strictly to manufacturer installation guidelines; inspect framing prior to installation.  
- Notify Project Superintendent of framing corrections at least two days prior to drywall installation.  
Taping Installation:  
- Three applications of joint compound required; minimum tape and joint width of 12”.  
- Finish all angles and corners throughout the house, including those in closets and pantries.  
Texture Requirements:  
- Texture walls and ceilings with a uniform knock down to adequately cover all seams; improper texture to be redone.  
- Protect adjacent areas from overspray and ensure all boxes and fixtures are left clean post-texture.

Garage Doors SOW - Mint  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install sectional garage doors complete with required fasteners and accessories.  
Installation Details:  Minor modification of rough opening for garage door is required.  
Hardware:  Sectional doors shall have standard track hardware with torsion springs of adequate capacity.  
Strut Requirement:  Provide a 2” strut above the top section at double doors.  
Weather Seal:  Synthetic rubber weather seal at the bottom of each door is mandatory.  
Door Specifications:  All metal doors must be a minimum of 26 gauge, with styles and sizes per Contract Specifications.  
Spring Type:  All doors shall be equipped with torsion type, oil tempered, stress relieved springs with a minimum cycle of 15,000.  
Hinge Specification:  Hinges must be heavy duty, 14 gauge.  
Panel Thickness:  Minimum of 2” thickness required for panels.  
Opener Installation:  Hard-wired, wall-mounted controllers must be installed flush, level, and plumb with concealed wiring. One opener per overhead door required.  
Testing:  Field test all installed doors, openers, and equipment before Contractor's final walkthrough.  
Safety Compliance:  Reversing safety system must meet Federal Law UL 325 standards.  
Emergency Feature:  Garage doors must include an emergency release if no other entry access exists.  
Painting:  Verify specified paint and paint LRV meet manufacturer's requirements if doors are to be painted.  
Referencing Document:  Additional requirements can be found in Exhibit C Contract Specifications.
110:
Windows & Doors - Scope of Work  
Contractor shall supply and install all windows, sliding glass doors, and exterior French doors in accordance with Title 24 energy calculations and project specifications.  
1. Product Specifications: All windows and doors must meet Title 24 performance ratings, including Low E and dual glazing requirements.  
2. STC Rating: Windows, glass doors, and exterior French doors to meet or exceed STC of 26.  
3. U-Values: Minimum U-Value ratings per NFRC and Title 24 must be provided.  
4. Compliance Checks: Report discrepancies between plans and elevations immediately to the Builder.  
5. Tempered Glass: Contractor is responsible for including all tempered windows per code, regardless of indication on plans.  
6. Labeling: Each unit must have a label with U-Value, SHGC, and STC ratings.  
7. Factory Finish: Window and door frames must be factory painted and assembled.  
8. Delivery: Windows to be delivered in complete packages per house by 9:00 AM on the delivery date, identified by target lot number.  
9. Warranty: Glazing, frames, and moving parts to be warranted against defects per manufacturer’s warranty per SB800.  
10. Installation Certificate: Provide Form CF-6R for energy compliance for each lot.  
11. Installation Criteria: Confirm openings are square, sills are level, and report any damaged products immediately to the Superintendent.  
12. Caulking: Required caulking per acoustical detail is included.  
13. Sealant: Use Rainbuster 450 polyurethane sealant for windows, applied around window flanges before and after unit installation.  
14. Flashing Installation: Use Rainbuster 420, attach with galvanized staples or nails, and ensure correct lapping for waterproofing.  
15. Cleaning: Clean excess sealant from site as work progresses using manufacturer-approved methods.

103:
Stucco (3-Coat) and Scaffolding  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install a complete exterior 3-Coat plaster system as per Stucco Institute specifications.  
Materials: All lath and plastering materials must adhere to current ICC approved applications; use Type I or II Omega cement conforming to ASTM C150.  
Sand Quality: Must be clean, free from loam, clay, silt, soluble salts, and organic matter; sampling to comply with ASTM C144 or C897.  
Water: Clean and potable without foreign matter for mixing and curing; no admixture without prior approval from Contractor.  
Installation: Use Top Industrial RainBuster 900 caulk/sealant; install 1-1/2” 17-gauge wire lath; no staples allowed.  
Penetrations: Flash all exterior penetrations prior to lath paper application; include Z-bar flashing at windows with stucco surrounds.  
Moisture Barrier: Install bituthane waterproof membrane; provide radiant barriers beneath building paper at gable ends per T-24.  
Building Paper: Two layers of building paper must include overlaps of a minimum of 2” horizontally and 6” vertically; install in shingle fashion.  
Curing: The brown coat shall be moist cured for 48 hours; minimum thickness of scratch coat shall be 3/8” and total of scratch and brown coat must be 7/8”.  
Scaffolding: Provide and maintain all scaffolding; it must comply with local and federal safety standards and be removed within 2 days of finish paint application.  
Protection: Mask all windows and doors during stucco operation; protect surrounding construction from damage due to stucco application.  
Clean Up: Install visqueen or equal at foundation to protect from overspray and stucco droppings; ensure all materials are protected during application.
99:

Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install a complete fire sprinkler system in accordance with approved plans and current codes.

Design Compliance: The fire sprinkler system shall be designed according to NFPA standards and the Jurisdictional Fire Code.  
Included Items: Contract includes piping, fittings, heads, check valve assemblies, drain valves, electric bells, horns, and strobes.  
Installation Precautions: Tradecontractor shall not install fire sprinklers in a manner that interferes with other trades.  
Plan Distribution: Deliver two complete sets of approved hydraulic calculations, drawings, and wiring diagrams; submit "as-built" drawings within 30 days of completion.  
System Type: Wet pipe system with sprinklers in living areas and garages per Code.  
Spare Parts: Tradecontractor to provide head box, three spare heads, and a pressure gauge riser.  
Material Specifications: Overhead lines to be Noveon CPVC "Blazemaster" or approved CPVC/metallic piping; no PEX approved.  
Structural Provisions: No structural members will be cut or drilled without Structural Engineer approval.  
Wiring: Each sprinkler riser shall have a vane type water flow switch wired to one interior and exterior bell provided by Tradecontractor.  
Testing and Access: Plumbing contractor to connect each fire sprinkler riser after Fire Department acceptance; fire-rated access doors required as per plans.  
Operation Manuals: O&M manuals to include system descriptions, shut down, and maintenance procedures delivered to the Superintendent.  
Acoustic Compliance: Contractor shall comply with requirements from plans and acoustical reports; include isolation felt pads and fire caulking.  
Fire Stopping: Approved sealant is Metacaulk 1000; fire blocks and stops must be installed as required.

Clean Construction Rough Clean SOW  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish all labor, material, equipment, and supervision for rough clean construction cleanup for Mint at Capital homes.  

Items Included:  All labor, material, equipment, taxes, and supervision for clean-up work inside homes, including garages.  
Recycling Requirements:  Adhere to governing agency recycling requirements; no toxic items, concrete, oil, or cleaning rags in dumpsters.  
Access Directions:  Follow jobsite Superintendent's directions for access across concrete; responsible for damage to improvements or structures.  
Weekend Work:  Staff required every Friday morning for marketing clean-up; ensure site is clear for weekend viewings.  
Debris Removal:  Remove debris from homes regardless of production pace; all debris to be placed in provided dumpsters.  
Interior Cleaning:  Clean inside and outside of houses and garages post-roof sheathing and roofing; clean before electrical/plumbing operations.  
Inspection Prep:  Sweep homes prior to Framing Inspections (minimum two sweeps); clean interior before insulation work.  
Prime Coat Prep:  Sweep homes post-finish trim installation for prime coat painting; coordinate schedules with Superintendent.  
Lot Cleanup:  Remove all debris from the lot before fine grading; rake and clean yards and fence lines post-installation of fencing.  
Final Clean Requirements:  Sweep, scrape, and pressure wash surfaces as required; ensure proper removal of all construction-related debris.  
Additional Cleans:  Authorized additional cleans for interior and exterior as needed for trash and debris removal.  
Project Specifications Date: 06/30/2026.  Refer to Exhibit C Contract Specifications for more requirements.

Insulation SOW - Mint  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall supply and install foam, batt, and blown insulation per Title 24 requirements.  
Cost Code: INSULATE - Insulation  
Insulation Types:  Foam, batt, and blown insulation to be installed.  
Quality Standards: HERS verified Quality Insulation Installation (QII) methods; formaldehyde-treated materials prohibited.  
Installation Phases: Pre-Insulation, Polycel after Rough Electrical, Batt Insulation, Blown Insulation.  
Installation Requirements: Insulation to avoid compression around electrical boxes and ensure proper venting, with unfaced insulation fitting tightly.  
Areas to Insulate: Attic access, cantilever sections per Title 24, above fire sprinkler pipes if required, and inaccessible areas as directed.  
Ventilation: Openings separating conditioned and unconditioned spaces must be sealed per California Energy Code; insulation must not block vents.  
Certification: Provide three copies of insulation certification documenting R-value, thickness, density, and material type used.  
Documentation: Complete CF-6R Insulation Certificates for each lot, submitting timely to Project Superintendent; register with CalCERTS if required.  
Fire Safety: Firestop mineral wool and draft stops included as per requirements, with fire-rated caulking where necessary.  
Application Conditions: Caulking to be applied over clean, debris-free surfaces; no application of polycel at vinyl windows.

349:
Railings Interior SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install stair rail system, aluminum powder-coated components, and glass railing system as specified.  
Cost Codes: RAILINT – Railings Interior, RAILINGL – Railings Glass - Option.  
Material Specifications: All materials shall be unfinished MDF; iron railings and balusters to be prefinished, powder-coated.  
Installation Requirements: It is the Tradecontractor’s responsibility to ensure installations meet all code requirements.  
Fasteners: Countersink all bolts, screws, and nails; conceal joints with plugs sanded flush.  
Connections: Rail-to-newel connections by lag or newel bolts; securely attach railings to surrounding construction with max movement of 1/4” under a 25-pound load.  
Baluster Installation: Balusters and newels must be plumb and spaced uniformly; splicing at a minimum of 8’.  
Glass Railing: 3/8” to ½” fully tempered safety glass must comply with ASTM C1048, with polished edges and dubbed corners.  
Anchors: Must be stainless steel, capable of sustaining loads with a safety factor of 4 per ASTM E488.  
Installation Tolerances: Max variation from plumb shall be ¼”; max offset for every 50 feet of railing is ¼”, non-accumulative.  
Cleaning Instructions: Wipe surfaces with a moistened cloth only; no abrasive or acid/alkaline cleaners allowed.  
Deficiencies: All work not meeting specifications must be corrected at no additional cost to the owner.  
Specifications Date: 07/02/2026; refer to Exhibit C for additional requirements.
234:
1. Railings Interior SOW - Grant  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install stair railing system and glass railing system as specified in the plans and Contract Specifications.  
Cost Codes: RAILINT – Railings Interior, RAILINGL – Railings Glass - Option.  
Material Specifications: All materials shall be unfinished MDF, sanded and ready to paint; iron railings and balusters shall be prefinished, powder-coated.  
Installation Requirements: Railings shall not move more than 1/4" under a 25-pound force; all connections must be concealed with plugs and sanded flush.  
Baluster Spacing: Spacing between balusters and newels shall be uniform; rail-to-newel connections shall use lag or newel bolts.  
Erection Tolerances: Maximum variation from plumb for railings is 1/4”; maximum offset for every 50-foot segment is 1/4", non-accumulative.  
Glass Specifications: Glass railing systems to be 3/8” to ½” fully tempered safety glass compliant with ASTM C1048; anchors must support loads with a safety factor of 4, tested per ASTM E488.  
Installation Execution: Installers must be qualified; components must be plumb, securely anchored, and free from distortion.  
Cleaning: Railings must be cleaned with a moistened cloth only; no abrasive or acid/alkaline cleaning agents are allowed.  
Deficiency Correction: Any deficiencies in work must be corrected at no additional cost to the owner; compliance with Exhibit C Contract Specifications is required.
82:
Railings Interior SOW - Mint  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install internal stair rail systems and components per specifications and plans.  
Cost Code: RAILINT - Railings Interior; RAILINGL - Railings Glass - Option  
Installation Requirements: All installations must meet applicable code requirements and ensure railings do not move more than 1/4" under a 25-pound horizontal force.  
Material Specifications: Unfinished MDF must be sanded and ready for paint; all iron railings and balusters to be prefinished, powder-coated.  
Connection Details: Rail-to-newel connections shall use lag or newel bolts; all bolts, screws, and nails must be countersunk and concealed with smooth, flush plugs.  
Spacing: Balusters and newels must be true, plumb, and uniformly spaced.  
Glass Railing System: Install tempered safety glass (3/8” to ½”) with polished edges per ASTM C1048; stainless steel anchors to sustain loads with safety factor of 4 per ASTM E488.   
Erection Tolerance: Maximum ¼” variation from plumb and true alignment offset for every 50-foot length of railing.  
Installation Process: Installation by qualified installers according to manufacturer's instructions; use of certified welders for any field welding.  
Cleanup: Railings and infill panels must be cleaned without abrasive cleaning agents; deficiencies must be corrected at no additional cost to owner.  
Final Notes: Refer to Exhibit C Contract Specifications for additional requirements.

2. Landscape and Concrete Installation  
Scope of Work: Contractor shall furnish all labor, materials, equipment, and incidentals for complete installation of landscape finish grading and various concrete surfaces as specified.  
Finish Requirements: Surface retarder shall be Lithochrome concrete surface retarder or equal; broom finish shall be uniform using a manila hemp bristle broom.  
Material Specifications: ABS Schedule 40 plastic drain pipe conforming to ASTM C-33; aggregate shall be 'San Fernando Stone'.  
Concrete Mix: Class A concrete shall contain not less than 5 sacks of cement per cubic yard and achieve a minimum cylinder strength of 2,500 psi in 28 days in accordance with ASTM C-39.  
Expansion Joints: Must be installed at 20’ o.c. for slabs and are to be tooled score joints of 1/2” deep as shown on drawings.  
Brick Paving: All brick paving shall be installed in accordance with governing authorities; maximum deviation from straight line shall not exceed 1/8 inches in 10 feet.  
Trench Depth: Bottom of trench must be smooth and continuous to facilitate flow, with a minimum pitch of 1/4" per foot unless otherwise indicated.  
Inspection: Surface conditions must be inspected to confirm proper installation of adjacent work before finishing begins.  
Curing Process: Newly placed concrete shall be kept wet for the first seven days post-placement.  
Compaction Requirement: Compaction by tamping must be done in layers not exceeding 8 inches in loose depth.

Windows & Installation SOW - Grant  
Scope of Work: Contractor shall supply and install all windows, sliding glass doors, and exterior French doors as per Title 24 energy efficiency standards and project specifications.

Low E Requirement: Minimum Low E Windows & Doors compliant with Title 24 standards.  
Glazing: Dual Glazed per California Title 24; minimum STC of 26 and U-Values per NFRC, including identification labels for U-Value, SHGC, and STC ratings.  
Security: Install security hardware per local ordinances; all installations must meet city requirements.  
Delivery: Confirm delivery schedules with Project Superintendent; complete packages due by 9:00 AM on delivery date.  
Installation Preparation: Check openings for size, square, and level before installation; report damaged material upon receipt.  
Sealant Application: Use Rainbuster 450 polyurethane sealant with specific application dimensions (3/8” to 1/2”) and follow detailed tooling instructions.  
Flashing: Install Rainbuster 420 flashing horizontally and vertically in accordance with specified measurements; do not fasten bottom edge of sill flashing.  
Completion Documentation: Provide a completed Installation Certificate, Form CF-6R, for each lot to demonstrate compliance with energy calculations.  
Warranty: Glazing, frames, and moving parts must be warranted against defects as per manufacturer’s specifications.  
Recessed Installation: Follow Top Industrial Rainbuster’s procedures for recessed windows with specified flashing dimensions and installation methods.  
Packaging: Mark each window package with target lot number and ensure unloading according to Project Superintendent’s instructions.  
Tempering: Include all tempered windows required by code, regardless of indication on plans; report discrepancies to Builder.

219:
Insulation and Installation  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install insulation according to current Title 24 requirements and specified methods.  
Installation Phases:  Insulation to be installed in pre-insulation, post-electrical rough, batt insulation, and blown insulation phases.  
Material Prohibition:  Formaldehyde-treated insulation is prohibited.  
Quality Standards:  HERS verified Quality Insulation Installation (QII) methods are required if per Title 24.  
Insulation Fit:  Unfaced insulation shall fit tightly to the top, bottom, and sides of the insulated cavity.  
Attic Requirements:  All insulation batts at roofline must be cut around attic vents; no insulation shall block vents.  
Certification:  Supply Contractor with three insulation certification copies showing R-value, thickness, density, and type for each area insulated.  
Sealing Requirements:  All openings separating conditioned and unconditioned spaces must be sealed per California Energy Code.  
Fire Safety:  Include firestop mineral wool and draft stops where required; fire-rated caulking to be used as necessary.  
Documentation:  CF-6R Insulation Certificates must be completed for each lot and submitted to Project Superintendent in a timely manner.  
Installation Security:  Insulation must be secured to withstand normal wind exposure in the project location.

268:
1. Solar Installation Specifications  
Scope of Work: Contractor shall install rooftop-mounted photovoltaic (PV) systems for the Grant at Capital 4-story walk-ups.  
Electrical: All wiring must be properly supported and installed in accordance with relevant codes. Roof-mounted wiring must be permanently held off the roof surface.  
Structural: Solar panels to be mounted using UltraRail system by SnapNrack. Mounting feet to be staggered on adjacent framing members to distribute load.  
Anchors: Mounting anchors shall be 5/16" lag screws with a minimum penetration of 2 1/2" into the roof framing.  
Load Impact: Proposed PV system adds 3.0 psf to the roof framing system. Roof live load is 20 psf typical.  
Wind Speed: Design for wind speed of 92 mph with exposure category C.  
Building Spec: Occupancy group R3/U, construction type V/B, 4 stories.  
System Components: Enphase IQ8HC-72-M-DOM-US inverter (@208V); Hanwha Q.PEAK DUO BLK ML-G10+ 410W solar modules.  
Energy Storage System: Optional Enphase IQ 5P (208V), 5kWh, 3.33kW.  
Governing Codes: Compliance with 2022 CA Building, Energy, Electrical, Fire, Green, Mechanical, Plumbing, and Residential Codes.

356:
Stucco (3-Coat) - Scope of Work  
Contractor shall furnish and install a complete exterior 3-Coat plaster system in accordance with specified standards and materials.  
Materials: All lath and plastering materials must meet Stucco Institute specifications; Type I or II Omega cement must conform to ASTM C150; sand must comply with ASTM C144 or C897.  
Installation: Supply and install 1-1/2” 17-gauge wire lath with no staples; corners and arches to be reinforced as per plans.  
Waterproofing: Supply and install bituthane waterproof membrane; flash all exterior penetrations including windows and doors.  
Building Paper: Two layers of building paper required, with minimum laps of 2” horizontal and 6” vertical; stagger fasteners for double layer installation.  
Curing: Brown coat to be cured for 48 hours and set for 7 days before applying finish coat; maintain moisture during curing process.  
Finishing: Scratch and brown coat minimum thickness must be 7/8”; finish coat to be standard base color unless otherwise specified.  
Scaffolding: Provide and maintain all necessary scaffolding, conforming to Cal OSHA and Federal OSHA standards; remove within 2 days post-paint application.  
Protection: Mask all windows and doors during stucco operation; protect adjacent construction from damage due to stucco application.  
Repair: Contractor to repair any cracks or imperfections caused by poor workmanship or improper curing.

284:
Stucco (3-Coat) and Scaffolding Scope of Work  
Contractor shall furnish and install a complete exterior 3-Coat plaster system, adhering to ICC approved applications and materials.  
Materials: All lath and plastering materials per Stucco Institute specifications.  
Cement: Use Type I or II Omega cement complying with ASTM C150.  
Sand: Must conform to ASTM C144 or C897; clean and free from contaminants.  
Wire: Supply and install 1-1/2” 17-gauge wire lath, with no staples allowed.  
Membranes: Install all bituthane waterproof membrane and Z-bar flashing as specified.  
Curing: Brown coat must be moist cured for 48 hours; total thickness of scratch and brown coat should be 7/8” minimum.  
Sealants: Use Top Industrial RainBuster 900 for joints; caulk around penetrations and frames.  
Scaffolding: Provide and maintain all scaffolding in compliance with OSHA standards; must not be placed against gutters.  
Protection: Mask all windows and doors; install visqueen to prevent overspray and stucco droppings.  
Coordination: Coordinate with masonry contractor for lath and coat behind masonry veneers; clean all excess materials post-application.  
Cracking: Repair any cracks caused by poor workmanship to the contractor’s satisfaction.  
Foam Trim: Expanded Polystyrene (EPS) foam with designs as per elevation plan.  
Fasteners: Staggered fasteners for double layer of 1-hr building paper installation.

278:
Fire Alarm SOW - Grant  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish, install, inspect, test, maintain, and certify the fire alarm systems as outlined.

Cost Code: FIALARM - Fire Alarm  
System Standards: Work shall conform to current NFPA 72 and equipment manufacturers’ recommendations.  
Complete System: Includes control panel, silent expansion module, low voltage wiring (if required), mini horns, water flow switch tie-in, PIV tie-in, smoke detectors, pull stations, and exterior bells.  
City Approval: Contractor responsible for understanding and adhering to all City and Fire Department requirements; additional costs for missed items not reimbursable.  
Design Review: Contractor shall design and submit the fire alarm system for City’s Fire Department approval, including associated fees.  
Material Compliance: All materials must be California State Fire Marshall and UL approved; all exterior equipment must be water-tight, with sealed penetrations.  
Inspection: Visual inspections will confirm system completeness; each device must be activated to ensure functionality.  
Testing Requirements: All tests and inspections required by the City’s Fire Department must be performed.  
Structural Penetrations: Require prior approval from a Structural Engineer before cutting or drilling.  
Electrical Coordination: Contractor must furnish all electrical components and coordinate installations with electrical and fire sprinkler contractors.  
Framing Standards: Notching, drilling, or cutting must be neat, adhering to structural engineer’s guidance, with all work conforming to code and approved plans.  
Acoustical Measures: Isolation pads and fire-stopping measures must comply with plans, acoustic reports, and codes using approved products like Metacaulk 1000.  
Fire/Sound Assembly: Inclusion of fire blocks and caulking in necessary locations, regardless of drawing specifications.  
Core Drilling: Includes layout, installation, and fire-stopping for penetrations through fire-rated and acoustic-rated walls (including post-tension decks).  
Project Specifications Date: 07/01/2026; refer to Exhibit C Contract Specifications for additional requirements.

HVAC Sheetmetal - SOW Document  
Contractor shall furnish and install heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems and sheet metal components according to the specified conditions.  
Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning: All equipment must deliver conditioned air adequately per room; complaints regarding system performance shall be directed to the Tradecontractor.  
Duct Requirements: All supply ducts must be insulated to a minimum installed level of R-6 and sealed as per ACM Manual; duct systems must comply with N.F.P.A. 90A and 90B, HUD and FHA approved.  
Thermostat: Furnish and install a programmable thermostat complying with Title 24 requirements, including low voltage wiring.  
Exhaust Systems: Provide and install jacks and vents for exhaust fans, dining hood systems, and ensure proper connection to plumbing contractor for condensate lines.  
Testing: Conduct testing, balancing, and start-up of the heating and/or air conditioning system prior to occupancy.  
Installation Certificate: Submit a completed Installation Certificate, Form CF-1R, for compliance with energy calculations.  
Air Distribution: Design per ACCA Manual J with airflow within +/- 10% of calculations; system pressure balance should not exceed +/- 3 pascals.  
Sheet Metal Specifications: All exterior sheet metal must be minimum 26 ga. galvanized and acid etched; include Z-bar flashing at required locations.  
Gutter Installation: Install rain gutters to completely drain roofing systems, with downspout supports every 5 feet and at every joint.  
Additional Components: Provide louvered sheet metal gable vents and foundation vents with screens; install metal flashing for masonry veneer where required.  
Fire/Sound Assembly: Comply with acoustical report, code, and local jurisdictions for fire and sound isolation measures.  
Project Specifications Date: 07/01/2026; refer to Exhibit C for further requirements.

Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install a complete fire sprinkler system in accordance with approved plans and current codes.  
Design: Fire sprinkler system shall adhere to NFPA standards and comply with the Jurisdictional Fire Code.  
System Components: Includes piping, fittings, heads, flow switches, check valves, drain valves, and exterior electric alarms.  
Plan Distribution: Deliver two complete sets of calculations, drawings and wiring diagrams to be stamped by the municipality.  
Installation: Wet pipe system required in living areas and garages per code; no cutting or drilling of structural members without Structural Engineer approval.  
Sprinkler Positions: Pendant sprinklers to be installed with concealed piping above sheetrock ceilings and aligned with ceiling fixtures.  
Quick Response Heads: Residential quick response heads in living areas; other areas have pendant/upright quick response heads as approved by the Fire Department.  
Riser Requirements: Each riser to have a vane type water flow switch and a minimum 1” globe valve for testing and drainage.  
Access: Include a Knox box per fire riser closet door and fire-rated access doors at riser locations if required.  
Inspections: Tradecontractor is responsible for scheduling and participating in Fire Department inspections; costs to be covered by the Tradecontractor.  
Operation Manuals: Provide operation and maintenance manuals, delivered to the Superintendent, detailing system description, shut down, and maintenance procedures.  
Fire Sound Assembly: Compliance with plans and acoustical report; includes felt pad isolation and installation of fire-stopping materials.  
Sealants: Metacaulk 1000 Firestopping Sealant required, with no substitutes allowed without approval.  
Fire Stops: Installation of fire blocks and caulking as necessary, regardless of architectural drawings.

General Requirements: All work to comply with N.E.C., local building codes, and other applicable laws; changes in specs require prior written approval.  
As-Built Drawings: Provide red-line, As-Built drawings showing all locations and circuiting after completion.  
Load Calculations: Submit load calculations prior to setting panels for each plan.  
Testing: Conduct tests to verify work is properly installed; all circuits must be operable before final acceptance.

Temp Power for Multi-Family: Coordinate switch gear install before shear inspection; provide a 50amp breaker and SO cord.  
Service Sleeves: Supply and install all underground service sleeves and sweeps as required by local utility.  
Electrical Wiring: All items must be U.L. listed, including service entrance panel and grounding system as per N.E.C. standards.  
Wiring Specifications: Use 12-gauge copper wire for 110V/20-amp circuits and 14-gauge for 110V/15-amp circuits; all receptacles must be U.L. listed.  
Smoke Detectors: Install interconnected smoke detectors and carbon monoxide detectors with battery backup as per code.  
Lighting: Primary kitchen and toilet lighting to be high efficacy per Title 24; all recessed lights must meet JA8 standards.  
Coordination: Work collaboratively with Plumbing, Framing, Fire Sprinkler, and HVAC trades to maximize efficiency.  
Fireproofing: Seal all firewall penetrations with fireproof caulking and install fire-rated assembly as required.  
Fasteners: Use corrosion-resistant screws on all exterior panels and outlets.  
Outlets: Ensure all outlets are installed per plan and comply with Title 24 standards.

295:
Structural Calculations and Design  
The contractor shall provide complete structural calculations for the Reserve at Capital residential buildings in Fremont, CA.  

Project Overview:  The project consists of two building types (4-plex and 7-plex) with townhouse-style units containing a garage on the ground floor and living areas on the upper two floors.  
Initial Submittal Date:  7/14/2025  
Revised Date:  11/13/2025  
Job Number:  HS24031  

Gravity System:  Loads are supported through plated wood trusses at roof level and manufactured wood I-joists at floors; framing on light-framed wood bearing walls.  

Design Load Summary:  
Roof Loads:  Total Dead Load (DL) 20 psf, Live Load (LL) 20 psf  
Floor Loads:  Total DL 15 psf, LL 40 psf  
Roof Seismic DL:  31 psf  
Floor Seismic DL:  35 psf  

Foundation:  Building loads are supported on a post-tensioned (PT) slab designed per recommendations from the project soils report.  

Lateral Force Resisting System:  Wind design uses ASCE 7 chapter 27; seismic design employs the Equivalent Lateral Force procedure from ASCE 7 chapters 11 and 12.  
Materials for resistance:  Wood shearwalls and Simpson Steel Strong-walls.  
Anchorage:  Metal strap holdowns at floors and metal holdowns at foundation level.  

Building Geometry:  
Building ID (4-Plex): Units 4, Width 88.5 ft, Depth 50 ft, Area 4200 sq ft  
Building ID (7-Plex): Units 7, Width 149 ft, Depth 49 ft, Area 6815 sq ft  
Number of Stories:  3  

Unit Types and Weights:  
Unit ID 1: Width 21 ft, Depth 48.3 ft, Seismic Weight 35 psf  
Unit ID 2: Width 21 ft, Depth 49.9 ft, Seismic Weight 35 psf  
Unit ID 3: Width 21 ft, Depth 48.9 ft, Seismic Weight 35 psf

364:
Sound Isolation Review - 25-081 Reserve at Capital  
Scope of Work: This report outlines the sound isolation requirements and recommendations for the Reserve at Capital residential development in Fremont, CA.

Regulatory Standards: Section 1206 of the 2022 State Building Code mandates sound transmission class (STC) ratings of not less than 50 and impact insulation class (IIC) ratings of not less than 50 for interior walls and floor-ceiling assemblies.

Air-borne Sound: Walls and floor-ceiling assemblies between dwelling units must have an STC of ≥ 50 (ASTM E90) or a Normalized Noise Isolation Class (NNIC) of ≥ 45 (ASTM E336).

Structure-borne Sound: Floor-ceiling assemblies must achieve an IIC rating of ≥ 50 (ASTM E492) or a Normalized Impact Sound Rating (NISR) of ≥ 45 (ASTM E1007).

Supplemental Standards: Recommended STC and IIC ratings for diverse acoustic quality levels are:  
- Minimum Quality: STC 55, IIC 55  
- Medium Quality: STC 60, IIC 63-67  
- High Quality: STC 65, IIC 73-77  

Design Considerations: Designs should incorporate a 3-5 dB margin of safety above minimum code levels to ensure effective sound isolation. 

Space Considerations: Impact sound insulation is not required for assemblies over non-habitable spaces, such as garages.

Quality Expectations: High-quality condominiums should provide sound isolation comparable to detached single-family residences to meet owner satisfaction.

Construction Guidelines: Penetrations for utilities must be sealed or insulated to maintain sound ratings; entrance doors must be tight-fitting.
266:
1. Sound Isolation Review Document  
2. Contractor shall provide recommendations for interior sound isolating building assemblies for the Grant residences.

Regulatory Compliance: The sound transmission class (STC) shall not be less than 50 for walls and floor-ceiling assemblies, as per Section 1206.2 of the 2022 State Building Code; Normalized Noise Isolation Class (NNIC) of not less than 45 if field tested per ASTM E336.  
Structure-Borne Sound: Impact insulation class (IIC) must be a minimum of 50 where tested in accordance with ASTM E492 or a Normalized Impact Sound Rating (NISR) of 45 if field tested per ASTM E1007.  
Supplemental Standards: Recommended STC and IIC ratings for high-quality multifamily dwellings are STC 65 and IIC 73-77.  
Minimum Quality: For normal apartments, minimum quality requires STC and IIC ratings of 55.  
Medium Quality: For good apartments and normal condominiums, the recommended STC is 60 and IIC is 63-67.  
High Quality: For high-quality condominiums, the recommended STC is 65 and IIC is 73-77.  
Design Margin: A 3 to 5 dB design margin of safety is recommended to ensure compliance and resident satisfaction.  
Construction Assemblies: All penetrations in assembly shall be sealed, lined, and insulated to maintain required ratings; exceptions apply to tight fitting entrance doors.  
Project Specifics: Review details for assemblies in Grant at Capital building drawings dated 6-30-25 for additional sound attenuation measures.
97:
Interior Sound Isolation Review

Scope: Provide interior sound isolation recommendations for the Mint residences at 3440 Walnut Avenue, Fremont, CA.

Residential Interior Acoustical Criteria: The report complies with Section 1206 of the 2022 State Building Code for sound transmission.

Air-borne sound: Walls, partitions, and floor-ceiling assemblies must have a Sound Transmission Class (STC) of at least 50 or a Normalized Noise Isolation Class (NNIC) rating of 45.

Structure-borne sound: Floor-ceiling assemblies should have an Impact Insulation Class (IIC) rating of at least 50 or a Normalized Impact Sound Rating (NISR) of 45.

Exception: Impact sound insulation is unnecessary for floor-ceiling assemblies over non-habitable rooms.

Supplemental Standards: Minimum STC and IIC ratings of 50 do not guarantee acoustical privacy; higher standards are recommended based on quality classifications.

Quality Classifications: 
- Minimum Quality: STC 55, IIC 55 for normal apartments.
- Medium Quality: STC 60, IIC 63-67 for good apartments/condominiums.
- High Quality: STC 65, IIC 73-77 for high-quality condominiums.

Testing Standards: Sound ratings should be verified via ASTM E90 and ASTM E492 testing methods.

Design Margin: A 3 to 5 dB design margin of safety is recommended above minimum requirements for compliance.

Building Interior Sound Isolation Review: Evaluation of current proposed wall and floor/ceiling assemblies based on compliance with the State Building Code and the supplemental standards.

Scope of Work: Contractor shall provide complete structural calculations for the Mint at Capital (3-Story Condos) project.

General Project Information: Project consists of two building types (6-plex and 8-plex) with townhouse-style units featuring garages on the ground floor.  
Building Geometry: 3 stories comprising 6-plex and 8-plex configurations, with units measuring up to 109.5 ft in width and 48 ft in depth.  
Gravity System: Supported by plated wood trusses at the roof and I-joists at the floors, with post-tensioned slab design per soil report.  
Lateral Force-Resisting System: Wind design per ASCE 7 Chapter 27; seismic design follows ASCE 7 Chapters 11 and 12; wood shearwalls provide resistance, with Simpson High-Strength Wood Strong-walls at the first floor.  
Anchor Design: Metal strap holdowns at the third-to-second and second-to-first floor levels; metallic holdowns at foundation level.  
Load Summary: Total dead load (DL) for roof: 18.5 psf; total DL for floors: 18.0 psf; total live load (LL) for roof: 20.0 psf; total LL for floors: 40.0 psf.  
Seismic Loads: Seismic dead load for roof: 30.0 psf; seismic dead load for floors: 38.0 psf; overall design adheres to 2022 CBC.  
Post-Tensioned Slab: Designed individually for each unique building type.  
Gravity Loads: Framing supported on light-framed wood bearing walls, specifying wood beams and posts where required.  
Design Procedures: Structural calculations detailed for all building configurations, accounting for worst-case scenarios in loading and resistance.

Structural Calculations and Design Document  
Scope: This document provides structural calculations and design details for the Grant at Capital residential project in Fremont, CA.

General Project Information: The project includes two building types (Building 1: 6-plex; Building 2: 8-plex) with up to four unit types; units feature garages on the ground floor and living areas above.

Gravity System Design: Loads are supported by plated wood trusses at the roof and manufactured wood I-joists at floors; all are supported on wood bearing walls. Foundation: Designed using recommendations from the project soils report with a post-tensioned slab.

Lateral Force-Resisting System: Wind design follows ASCE 7 Chapter 27; seismic design follows ASCE 7 Chapters 11 and 12. Wood shearwalls resist lateral forces; Simpson High-Strength Wood Strong-Walls applied at first floor garage fronts.

Seismic Weight: Building 1 has a seismic weight of 44.35 psf (1st floor) to 32.38 psf (4th floor); Building 2 has 44.42 psf (1st floor) to 32.46 psf (4th floor).

Design Loads: Roof Dead Load: 20.0 psf; Floor Dead Load: 25.0 psf. Roofing load is 9.0 psf; flooring is 8.0 psf.

Unit Configuration: Building 1 consists of 3 units (Total area 15,205 sqft); Building 2 consists of 4 units (Total area 26,423 sqft).

Anchorage: Calculations for anchors include standard Abs & MASAs, Holddown Types (16 & 21), and additional calculations for holddowns without anchorage.

Overall Design Approach: One calculation package covers all buildings with gravity design on a unit basis, lateral loads designed per worst-case scenarios across configurations.

Design-Level Geotechnical Exploration for Capital Residential Development  

Scope of Work: Conduct a design-level geotechnical exploration to assess subsurface conditions for the proposed residential development in Fremont, California.  

1. Purpose: Provide geotechnical design recommendations based on site condition characterization.  
2. Site Location: 3440 Walnut Avenue, Fremont, California.  
3. Existing Structures: Detail on existing structures and site improvements was provided but not specified here.   
4. Proposed Development: Residential development as per project specifications.  
5. Previous Investigations: Notable past investigations include those by Lee & Praszker (1981), Cleary Consultants (1981), Dames & Moore (1989), and Earth Mechanics (2003-2004).  
6. Findings Summary:  
   - Geology: Site features typical alluvial deposits.  
   - Seismicity: Analyze potential seismic hazards, including ground shaking and liquefaction.  
7. Groundwater Conditions: Static and perched groundwater levels were evaluated and deemed significant to design considerations.  
8. Conclusions:  
   - Compressible Soil: Settlement analyses indicate areas of concern.  
   - Non-Engineered Fill: Identification of non-engineered fill requiring removal during site prep.  
   - Expansive Soil: Recommendations for managing expansive soil conditions.  
9. Recommendations:  
   - Earthwork: Detailed earthwork recommendations including demolition and site preparation methods.  
   - Fill Material: Acceptable fill materials and sources for backfill areas outlined.  
   - Compaction: Minimum compaction standards required for fill areas specified.  
10. Final Report: Document dated January 28, 2025, prepared for Brookfield Calwest Builders, LLC by ENGEO Incorporated.

Final Touches:  Spackle all trim materials and smooth splits; repairs post-painting must be repainted at contractor's expense.  
Window/Sill Plates:  Install two 2x4 plates at all window openings; ensure door heights align with plans and specifications.  
Soffits and Furred Walls:  Construct all required furred walls, soffits, and drop ceilings per Project Superintendent direction.  
Pot Shelves:  Install pot shelves sloped no less than 1/4” per foot.
209:
Framing Labor and Lumber SOW - Grant  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install all framing materials and perform rough carpentry in accordance with industry standards and project specifications.  
Cost Codes: FRMLABOR – Framing Labor; LUMFRAME – Framing Lumber; LUMPNL – Panelization; JOISTFLR – Joists-Floor; TRUSROOF – Trusses-Roof.  
Compliance: All framing shall meet current U.B.C. standards and be constructed plumb, level, and true.  
Materials: Supply and install structural and non-structural lumber, trusses, rough hardware, joists, sub-floor, beams, headers, sheathing, exterior trim, and all necessary blocking and backing.  
Flooring: Glue and screw plywood or OSB subfloor using a premium adhesive (Rainbuster No. 345) in accordance with manufacturer’s guidelines.  
Sealing: Seal exterior bottom plates with rope caulk or foam; caulk all eaves, windows, and relevant materials to ensure water-tightness.  
Post Installation: Install supports at sleepers, thresholds, and sliding glass doors; comply with all layout instructions provided to the concrete foundation contractor.  
Stair Construction: Construct and install both interior and exterior stairways, adhering to OSHA safety standards.  
Temporary Safety: Provide and maintain temporary wooden safety handrails and shoring for floor joists as required.  
Soffits and Furred Walls: Include all required walls, soffits, chases, and ceiling drops for compatibility with other trades as per Project Superintendent's direction.  
Final Touch: Ensure all trim is spackled and painted as needed; all door heights must match plans and specifications, with inaccuracies reported to the Project Superintendent.  
Shower Specifications: Install three 2x4 shower dams and ensure sloping to drain meets a minimum of 1/8" per foot.  
Window/Sill Plates: Install two flat 2x4 plates at all window openings as per plans.  
Critical Measurements: Prepare critical layout measurements for embedding hardware and coordinate with other trades on all rough openings.  
Tolerances: Confirm foundation meets 1/4” in 10’ tolerance before commencing framing work.
135:
Framing Labor and Lumber SOW - Mint
