350:
Construction Finish Clean SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall provide complete house cleaning services at Reserve at Capital in Fremont, CA.  

Cost Codes:  
CLNFINAL – Clean-Final  
CLNPWWSH – Clean-Power Wash  
CLNWNDWS – Clean-Windows  

Cleaning Agents:  
All glass to be cleaned with non-abrasive and non-corrosive cleaners.  
Sinks, tubs, and shower pans to be cleaned using non-abrasive methods.  
No toxic or harmful residual effects allowed from cleaning agents.  
Compliance with Federal OSHA, Cal OSHA, and EPA guidelines is mandatory.  

Rough Clean:  
Scheduled after hard surface flooring installation and prior to floor covering.  
Interior and exterior windows washed including frames and tracks.  
Bathrooms to be cleaned including shower pans and tubs.  
Interior of cabinets to be cleaned and debris removed.  

Final Clean-1:  
To occur after carpet installation and prior to buyer pre-carpet walk.  
All bathrooms cleaned, including tubs, sinks, and fixtures.  
Kitchen fixtures, floors, cabinets, and appliances to be cleaned inside and out.  
All interior and exterior cabinets vacuumed with no oil left on cabinets.  
Clean floor registers, heating ducts, fireplace glass doors, and hardware.  

Final Clean-2:  
Before buyer walkthrough and Homeowner Orientation.  
Windows, frames, and tracks to be cleaned; debris removed before maid service.  
Hardwood flooring must be swept or vacuumed prior to using Bona Floor Cleaner.  
Bona mop heads to be changed after every 100-150 sq. ft.  

Final Clean-3 FLUFF & BUFF:  
Before close of escrow clean.  
Vacuum house and address minor spots for cleanliness.  
Dust and clean surfaces including countertops and appliances.  
Cobwebs removed from all interior surfaces; all debris to be removed.  

Project Specifications Date: 06/30/2026  
Refer to Exhibit C Contract Specifications for additional requirements.  
Rough cleaning of exterior windows must occur after painting before scaffolding removal.
230:
Cleaning Services - Construction Finish Clean SOW  
Contractor shall provide comprehensive final cleaning services for new residential construction at Grant at Capital, Fremont, CA.  

Trade Specific Conditions:  
1. Labor and Materials: Contractor shall furnish all labor, materials, equipment, and supervision necessary for complete house cleaning.  
2. Equipment Access: Provide ladders, scaffolding, and extension poles to access all windows, both interior and exterior.  
3. Training: Labor force must be trained in window cleaning techniques and safe jobsite practices.  

CLEANING AGENTS:  
1. Non-Abrasive Cleaners: All glass to be cleaned using non-abrasive and non-corrosive cleaners.  
2. Safe Chemicals: Cleaning agents must not leave harmful residues and must comply with OSHA and EPA guidelines.  
3. Damage Liability: Any damage from products or tools will be the responsibility of the contractor.  

ROUGH CLEAN:  
1. Timing: Schedule immediately after hard surface flooring is installed.  
2. Window Cleaning: Clean all interior and exterior windows and sliding glass doors, ensuring manufacturer labels remain on tracks.  
3. Bathroom Cleaning: Thorough cleaning of all bathrooms, including tubs and shower pans.  
4. Cabinet Cleaning: Clean out the interior of cabinets.  

FINAL CLEAN-1:  
1. Post-Carpet Installation: To be completed after carpet installation and before the buyer pre-carpet walk.  
2. Bathroom Fixtures: Clean all bathroom fixtures including tubs, sinks, and toilets with disinfectants.  
3. Kitchen Details: Clean all kitchen fixtures, appliances (inside and out), and countertops.  
4. Cleaning Schedule: Vacuum, dust, and clean all interior spaces and hard flooring.  
5. Instruction Manuals: Place all manuals for the homeowner in a kitchen drawer.

FINAL CLEAN-2:  
1. Pre-Walkthrough Cleaning: To be completed before the buyer walkthrough and Homeowner Orientation.  
2. Windows and Floors: Clean all windows and ensure floors are vacuumed or swept prior to further cleaning.  
3. Fixture Polishing: Clean and polish faucets, door handles, and wipe down fixtures.  
4. Debris Removal: All debris must be removed to a designated area as instructed by the Project Superintendent.

FINAL CLEAN-3 FLUFF & BUFF:  
1. Close of Escrow Cleaning: To be done before the close of escrow.  
2. Comprehensive Vacuuming: Vacuum house and remove minor spots.  
3. Kitchen and Bathroom Final Cleaning: Clean all kitchen and bathroom fixtures thoroughly.  
4. Cobweb Removal: Ensure to remove cobwebs from all interior surfaces.  
5. Debris Management: All debris must be removed to a designated area by the Superintendent.  

Project Specifications Date: 07/01/2026  
1. Refer to Exhibit C for additional requirements.  
2. Rough cleaning of exterior windows must occur after painting and before scaffolding is removed for 3rd and 4th floor access.

310:
Roofing, All SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install roofing systems per elevations including tile, TPO, and metal, adhering to all relevant specifications and regulations.

Craft Codes:  
RFCMPASP – Roofing Comp/Asphalt  
RFMETAL - Roofing Metal  
RFTILE - Roofing Tile  
RFTPO – Roofing TPO  

Manufacturer’s Specifications:  All roofing to be installed per manufacturer’s specifications, current code, and TRI specifications.  
Wind Factors:  Roofing materials to meet high-wind requirements as necessary.  
Drainage:  Contractor responsible for proper drainage of all roofs.  
Fasteners:  Fasteners to meet U.B.C. requirements; must be corrosion resistant.  
Fall Protection:  Comply with CAL OSHA revised 15-foot trigger height regulations.  
Waste Disposal:  Onsite and offsite disposal of materials must align with E.P.A. requirements and local debris recovery programs; at least 50% debris diversion required.  
Leak Protection:  Assurance of 100% weathertight installation at time of felting.  
Roof Vents:  Supply and install O’Hagin roof vents as per plans; install 30lb. ASTM felt (4” head lap).  
Tile Roof Requirements:  Supply and install valley metal; weather-tight completion of felt required.  
Flat Roof Requirements:  Install fire-rated high-density roof board; ASTM D 6878 uniform TPO material; ensure all penetrations are sealed per manufacturer's specifications.  
Mechanically Fastened Installation:  Follow manufacturer's instructions for alignment and securing of roofing membrane; ensure watertight seams.
287:
Roofing, All SOW - Grant  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install roofing systems according to manufacturer’s specifications, local codes, and industry standards.  
Craft Code: RFCMPASP, RFMETAL, RFTILE, RFTPO.  
Roofing Installation:  Install roofing per elevation and ensure compliance with TRI specifications, current code, and high-wind requirements.  
Wind Considerations:  Tradecontractor to consider local wind factors and ensure all materials meet high-wind specifications.  
Drainage Responsibility:  Contractor is responsible for ensuring proper drainage of all roofs.  
Fasteners:  Use corrosion-resistant fasteners per U.B.C. requirements.  
Safety Compliance:  Adhere to CAL OSHA fall protection regulations for heights over 15 feet.  
Waste Disposal:  Onsite disposal of excess materials must comply with E.P.A. requirements; offsite disposal of roof tiles must adhere to local Construction & Demolition Debris Recovery Program, ensuring at least 50% diversion from landfills.  
Leak Protection:  Ensure roofs are 100% weathertight at time of installation.  
Tile Roof Requirements:  Install O’Hagin roof vents, 30lb. ASTM felt (4” head lap), wooden batts with hot-dipped galvanized nails, and seal all hips and ridges.  
Flat Roof Specifications:  Install fire-rated high-density roof board, ASTM D 6878 TPO membrane, and ensure proper installation of all accessories, including TPO boots and flashings.  
Membrane Installation:  Mechanically fasten roofing membranes per manufacturer’s instructions, ensuring watertight seams with hot-air welding and appropriate testing.  
Completion Timeline:  Roof to be weather-tight at completion of felt, protect from UV damage and ensure all prerequisites are verified before proceeding with inspections.
124:
Roofing, All SOW - Mint

Contractor shall furnish and install roofing systems as per manufacturer's specifications and local building codes for all roof types.  
Craft Code: RFCMPASP – Roofing Comp/Asphalt, RFMETAL - Roofing Metal, RFTILE - Roofing Tile, RFTPO – Roofing TPO.  
Roofing Installation: All roofing materials shall be installed to meet high-wind requirements typical for the area.  
Fasteners: Must be corrosion resistant and comply with U.B.C. requirements.  
Fall Protection: Comply with CAL OSHA regulations for fall protection at trigger height of 15 feet.  
Debris Disposal: Onsite disposal of materials in an EPA-compliant manner; offsite disposal of roof tiles must adhere to local Construction & Demolition Debris Recovery Program, diverting at least 50% of debris from landfills.  
Weather Protection: Ensure roofs are 100% weathertight at the completion of felting.  
Tile Requirements: Supply and install O’Hagin roof vents, one (1) layer 30lb. ASTM felt with 4” head lap, and ensure discolored tiles are replaced, not recolored.  
Flat Roof Installation: Supply and install fire-rated high-density roof board with TPO membrane; ensure all flashings and penetrations are properly sealed and installed per manufacturer's specifications.  
Seam Welding: Hot-air weld seams and test for continuity; repair any unsatisfactory conditions before proceeding.  
Final Inspection: Walk roof before final inspection to address cracked and broken tiles.   
Roof Vent Calculations: Tradecontractor must review and confirm calculations shown on plans.  
Loss Responsibility: Contractor reserves the right to relocate stored tiles at Tradecontractor’s expense if they are damaged while on the ground.
365:
Solar Panels SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install a solar electric system including solar panels, inverters, and monitoring equipment in accordance with all relevant codes and specifications.  
System Components:  Includes inverters, connectors, monitoring equipment, disconnects, electrical hardware, wiring, conduits, and labels per NEC codes.  
Warranties:  Minimum 10-year parts and labor warranty; minimum 20-year power output warranty.  
Permitting:  Tradecontractor shall obtain all required building permits before installation and submit a complete permit package stamped by a professional engineer.

Installation Compliance:  All work must meet local building codes, NEC, and manufacturer's specifications.  
Inspection Coordination:  Arrange for and attend all required inspections for final approval by the permitting agency and utility.  
Roof Attachment:  Installation to include proper attachment and flashing as per manufacturer requirements; must avoid installation of defective materials.  
Monitoring System:  Solar system shall include a web-based monitoring system requiring a high-speed internet connection if specified.  
Material Specifications:  Solar panels must have a Class A rating and include necessary underlayment and electrical components for a complete installation as specified.  
Electrical Materials:  Provide MET or FMC raceway for DC conductors and necessary internal wiring during rough-in phase.  
Installation Location:  Coordinate the locations for solar array and inverter installation with Project Superintendent.  
External Utilities:  If required by the electric utility, provide a visible area for the disconnect switch at least 24 inches from any gas line.  
BIPV Systems:  For Building Integrated Photovoltaics, install underlayment and flashing as specified by the manufacturer.

340:
Countertops and Wall Slabs SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install natural stone, engineered, and manufactured slab countertops and wall slabs as specified in the contract.  

Cost Codes: CTOPSS – Countertops Solid Surface; CTOPGQ – Countertops Granite/Quartz; WALLSLGQ – Wall Slabs Granite/Quartz; WALLSLSS – Wall Slabs Solid Surface.  
Material Thickness: All slabs shall be a minimum of 2cm unless otherwise specified.  
Backsplashes: Shall be tightly adhered to walls; no backsplash at cabinet returns within 24” of sink or adjacent to bathtubs.

Waterproofing: Install bituthane waterproofing membrane at vertical and horizontal corners of showers and tubs.  
Gap Specifications: Gaps between countertops and walls must not exceed 1/16”.  
Preparation: Inspect sinks, tubs, and shower pans prior to slab installation; report any defects.  
Protection: Contractor must protect completed work and respect local disposal requirements, ensuring 50% of debris is recycled.  
Compliance: All work shall conform to the UBC edition required by public authorities and manufacturer specifications.  
Care Instructions: Provide care instructions for use during buyer walk-throughs prior to closing and occupancy.  
Edge Details: Refer to Edge Detail Menu for standard and upgrade edge details applicable to specific installations.

346:
Landscaping SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall provide all labor, materials, tools, equipment, and transportation for landscaping work as specified for the Reserve at Capital development.  
General Notes:  Completion of work is subject to written acceptance by the City and Contractor.  
Site Conditions:  Tradecontractor accepts site conditions "as is" and agrees to complete all work per governing agencies' standards.  
Planting Provisions:  No deviations from the plans shall be allowed without prior written approval from Contractor and Landscape Architect.  
Damage Responsibility:  Tradecontractor must repair any damage to existing structures or utilities at their expense.  
Tree Protection:  Precautions must be taken to protect existing trees; any removal requires Contractor's consent.  
Conflict Notification:  Any conflicts with codes or regulations discovered must be reported to Contractor in writing.  
Security:  Tradecontractor is responsible for the security of their own materials and equipment left on site.  
Excluded Work:  Includes coring or cutting concrete, installation of construction meters, and costs related to water meters.  
Irrigation Requirements:  Tradecontractor shall install all landscape sprinkler work as per landscape drawings, including adjustments to heads to eliminate overspray.  
Code Compliance:  Irrigation installation in rights-of-way must adhere to local governing codes.  
Piping Specifications:  Minimum cover depths are 18" for main lines and 12" for sprinkler lines; piping must be PVC Schedule 40.  
Trench Requirements:  Trenches must be straight with clean bottoms, free from rocks or sharp objects.  
Installation Testing:  The entire irrigation system must be tested in the presence of the Project Superintendent before operation.  
Adjustments:  Head locations and arcs must be adjusted to ensure full coverage and prevent dry spots.  
Wiring:  Wiring for 110 volts is not included and shall be provided by others; automatic valves and controllers must be installed per plans and specifications.  
Warranty:  Compaction must prevent settling within a one-year warranty period.
344:
Pest Control SOW - Reserve  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install pest control measures for the Reserve at Capital, Fremont, CA.  
Soils Pre-Treatment: Vertical and horizontal barriers against subterranean termites to be established.  
Vertical Barrier: Treat both sides of footing using 2 gallons per 5 lineal feet after footing pour, prior to slab on grade.  
Critical Joints Treatment: All critical joints, plumbing, and electrical conduit penetrations treated at the same rate as vertical barrier.  
Horizontal Barrier: Apply at a rate of 1 gallon per 10 square feet simultaneously with vertical barrier treatment.  
Wood Pre-Treatment: Pre-treat the bottom 24” of all studs on exterior and interior walls.  
Timing of Wood Treatment: Application to occur after all wood and sheathing are in place, before insulation, mechanical systems, and electrical wiring installation.  
Saturation Condition: Treatment shall not be performed if wood is saturated.  
Project Specifications Date: 07/02/2026.  
Refer to Exhibit C: Contract Specifications for additional requirements.

Contractor shall perform quality inspections for the Reserve at Capital project to ensure compliance with applicable standards and specifications.  
1. Pre-Lath Waterproofing: Inspections conducted before the water resistive barrier application, focusing on proper installation of windows, doors, vents, and flashing.

4. Balcony Waterproofing: Inspections of waterproofing methods, material compliance, substrate preparation, and integration of drainage systems.  
5. Roof Dry-in and Low Sloping Roofing: Inspections pre-finish roofing material, assessing underlayment, penetrations, and integration of roofing components.

355:
Concrete Work - Scope of Work  
Contractor shall furnish and install concrete patios, driveways, and walkways at the Reserve at Capital, Fremont, CA.  
Layout: Layout flatwork corners.  
Piping: Provide and install all piping and sleeving under city sidewalks and driveways.  
Utility Boxes: Adjust and level all utility boxes to match finish grade.  
Concrete Specifications: Use 4” of 2500 PSI concrete over 4” of washed/crushed (3/4” dia.) base rock at 90% compaction for stoops and walks; driveways include #3 reinforcing bar @ 36” O.C.E.W.  
Mix Design: Concrete shall conform to ASTM C94 with not less than five (5) sacks of Portland Cement per cubic yard; minimum ultimate compressive strength of 2500 psi in 28 days.  
Finishing: Furnishing, placing, tooling, and broom finishing concrete flatwork; all flatwork to be sloped to avoid standing water.  
Curing: Concrete must be cured for a minimum of seven (7) days; applied curing and sealing compounds shall be compatible with the finish.  
Control Joints: Provide control joints spaced a maximum of eight feet (8’) and deep scored a minimum of ¾” deep.  
Expansion Joint Filler: Approved fillers to be “Sol-U-Board” or other pre-molded strips; submit sample for approval.  
Joint Tie Wire: Use 16 gauge black annealed tie wire.  
Control Joint Detail: Control joints shall be 1/4th the thickness of the slab; attention needed at re-entrance corners.  
Inspections: Conduct inspections as required; onsite disposal of excavation spoils where agreed between Tradecontractor and Contractor.

Testing:  All connected lines must be tested for proper operation.

363:
Plumbing - Scope of Work  
Contractor shall furnish and install all plumbing systems as per local jurisdiction requirements, including water supply, drainage, waste, and vent systems with specific acoustical and fire safety measures.  
General Requirements: Contractor must submit plumbing layouts for approval and coordinate with the Project Superintendent.  
Testing: All plumbing systems must be tested per code, with defects rectified at contractor's expense.  
Onsite Inspections: An onsite foreman is required to oversee plumbing inspections.  
Penetrations: All penetrations through firewalls must be sealed per local code; quickflash flashing is required at all exterior piping penetrations.  
Piping: All pipes must be sized to minimize noise and wrapped for soundproofing; no leaks are permitted in water supply, gas, and wastewater systems.  
Fire Safety: Compliance with fire assembly details and installation of fire blocks and caulking is a must, no alternates allowed.  
Underground Work: Trenching is required inside form boards, with a minimum of 4” of sand below and over sewer pipes; sewer piping must conform to ASTM F-628-93.  
Drain and Vent: ABS risers are needed in living areas; cleanouts must be placed per Sanitary District requirements with traffic-rated lids as necessary.  
Water Supply: Use PEX, Schedule 80 CPVC, or Type M copper piping; all water piping under concrete must be sleeved.  
Water Hammer Arrestors: Must be installed at refrigerator and washing machine connections.  
Hose Bibbs: Non-removable backflow prevention is required; frost-free hose bibbs are included.  
Final Inspection: After completion of all work, contractor must snake and camera all waste lines to ensure they are clear.  
Documentation: Video inspections of waste systems must be provided to the builder on USB.

361:
Exterior Deck Waterproofing - Deck Waterproofing, Plidek SOW

Scope of Work: Contractor shall furnish and install Pli-Dek waterproofing systems on all exterior above-grade decks and other specified areas.

Materials Installation: All materials must be installed according to manufacturer recommendations; any deviations will be the contractor's expense.

Deck System: Pli-Dek “F” system with a knock down finish is to be installed.

Substrate Requirements: All substrates must be structurally sound, with no contaminants; plywood substrates must be at least 5/8” thick, exterior grade, sloped ¼" per foot.

Plywood Details: Edges must be gapped 1/8", with maximum support spacing of 16” for AC grade plywood; tongue and groove plywood is acceptable.

Moisture Content: Substrates must have moisture content below 15%, verified with a moisture meter.

Installation Verification: Contractor must ensure deck meets all substrate requirements and drains properly.

Waterproofing System: The installation consists of a two-stage Pli-Dek system including a metal lath and underlayment followed by a three-coat waterproof membrane.

Metal Lath: Must be rust-free and primed with Primer #101; install lath using offset staples in a checker pattern 8” on centers.

Coat Application: First coat forms waterproof seal and adheres fiberglass mat; second coat provides skid resistance; final coat seals aggregates.

Finish Coat: Apply Pli-Dek #3 at approximately 125 sq. ft. per gallon, allowing 8 hours to dry and 72 hours before heavy objects can be placed.

Final Colors: Colors for each home will follow the attached Exterior Color Scheme. 

Flashings and Drains: All metal flashings, deck drains and scuppers are to be provided per plans; gutters will be handled by others.

248:
Exterior Deck Waterproofing - Exhibit “B” Scope of Work  
Contractor shall furnish and install Pli-Dek waterproofing system on exterior above grade decks.  
Materials: All materials must be installed per manufacturer’s recommendations with full responsibility for non-compliance.  
System: Pli-Dek “F” system with knock down finish; acrylic polymer cementitious underlayment with metal lath reinforcement and three coats of waterproof membrane.  
Substrates: Must be structurally sound, at least 5/8” thick exterior grade plywood, sloped ¼" per foot, and gapped 1/8" at edges.  
Fastening: 8 penny ring shank nails on maximum 6” centers; tongue and groove plywood acceptable; OSB/chipboard prohibited.  
Moisture Content: Must be below 15%, verified with moisture meter.  
Installation Process: Two stages including installation of metal lath and underlayment followed by three coats of waterproof membrane.  
Texture Coat: Apply skid-resistant texture coat (Pli-Dek #2) at approximately 75 sq. ft. per gallon.  
Finish Coat: Apply finish coat (Pli-Dek #3) at approximately 125 sq. ft. per gallon; allow 24 hours curing before foot traffic and 72 hours for heavy objects.  
Metal Flashings: All metal flashings, deck drains, and scuppers to be provided and installed per plans.  
Project Specifications: Refer to Exhibit C for additional requirements.
180:
Exterior Deck Waterproofing, Plidek SOW - Mint  
Contractor shall furnish and install Pli-Dek waterproofing systems on all exterior above grade decks as specified.  
Materials: All materials must be installed per manufacturer’s recommendations; Tradecontractor is responsible for removal and replacement of any deviations.  
Deck Underlayment System: Pli-Dek “F” system with knock down finish to be used.  
Substrate Requirements: Substrates must be structurally sound, minimum 5/8” thick exterior grade plywood, sloped ¼” per foot, with proper drainage.  
Installation: 
1. Install metal lath reinforcement and underlayment as per specifications.  
2. Apply waterproof membrane in three coats: initial seal coat, skid-resistant texture coat, and final finish coat.  
3. Metal lath should be rust-free and when applying sealants, primed with Primer #101.  
4. Properly treat joints (tooled, primed, caulked) with approved sealant.  
5. Verify drainage and slope of decks; responsibility for proper sloping lies with Tradecontractor post-installation commencement.  
Texture Coating: Skid-resistant texture applied at 75 sq. ft. per gallon; finish coat at 125 sq. ft. per gallon.  
Curing Times: Allow 24 hours for cure before foot traffic; 72 hours for heavy loads post-final coat.  
Flashings: All required metal flashings, deck drains, and scuppers to be provided as per plans.  
Project Specifications Date: 07/01/2026; refer to Exhibit C for additional specifications.

Fire Protection Document  
Scope: Contractor shall install a wet type automatic fire sprinkler system in accordance with NFPA 13R and applicable codes.  
Project Address: Fremont, CA  
Occupancy Type: R-2  
Construction Type: V-A  
Number of Stories: 3  
Actual Gross SF: 8,658 SF  
Authority Having Jurisdiction: Fremont Fire Department  
Design Standards: NFPA 13R 2022 Edition, 2022 California Building Code, 2022 California Fire Code, 2022 California Mechanical Code, 2022 California Electrical Code, 2022 California Plumbing Code  
System Components: All materials to be U.L tested and/or F.M. approved for fire sprinkler use.  
Pipe Type: Residential floors' piping shall be BlazeMaster CPVC plastic with CPVC fittings; main riser to be EddyFlow (or equal) black pipe with grooved fittings.  
Pipe Standards: Steel pipe shall meet ASTM A135 standards with a CRR value of 1.0 or greater.  
Installation Criteria: Sprinkler locations and spacing per NFPA 13R and manufacturer's listing; all hangers to be designed and installed in accordance with NFPA 13R.  
Water Supply Data: Static Pressure: 59 PSI, Residual Pressure: 57 PSI, Flow: 1030 GPM, Source: ACWD.  
Hydrostatic Test: 200 PSI for 2 hours; witnessed by fire inspector.  
Heat Requirements: Adequate heat (40°F and above) shall be maintained in all wet pipe areas to prevent system bursting.  
Spare Parts: Spare sprinkler heads and wrench shall be provided by contractor.  
Flushing Requirements: Underground piping must be flushed and tested by the installing contractor before connection to the fire sprinkler system.  
Testing Requirements: Flushing and testing of all system piping shall be conducted prior to connection.
378:
Fire Protection Bid Document  
Scope of Work: Installation of a wet type automatic fire sprinkler system at Reserve 7-Plex in Fremont, CA.  
Design Standard: Installation shall comply with NFPA 13R 2022 Edition and 2022 California Building Code.  
Occupancy Type: R-2; Construction Type: V-A; Number of Stories: 3; Actual Gross SF: 14,493 SF.  
Pipe Material: Main riser and cross mains shall be EDDYFLOW black pipe with grooved fittings; residential floor piping to be BlazeMaster CPVC plastic with CPVC fittings.  
Hydraulic Test: Hydrostatic test at 200 PSI for 2 hours must be witnessed by the fire inspector.  
Sprinkler Heads: Viking VK494 with 4.9 K-factor, concealed pendent type; Viking VK3021 with 5.6 K-factor, pendent type; Viking VK327 with 4.2 K-factor, upright type.  
Sprinkler Density: Light hazard design density shall be 0.050 GPM/SQFT; for garages, 100 GPM hose stream demand.  
Installation Requirements: All hangars must be spaced and installed as per NFPA 13R; verify finish ceiling elevation prior to sprinkler head installation.  
Flushing and Testing: Flushing of underground piping must be performed by the installing contractor before the connection.  
Alarm Installation: To be done by others; 24-hour supervision required from a third-party contractor.  
Heat Prevention: Building owner must maintain adequate heat (40°F and above) in all wet pipe areas to prevent bursting.  
Seismic Bracing: Flexible couplings required within 24 inches of all risers; longitudinal sway bracing max 15 feet beyond end of the pipe.

329:
Concrete Foundations - Concrete Foundation SOW

Scope of Work: Contractor shall furnish and install concrete footings, garage slabs, and associated embedded items in compliance with structural plans and soils report.

Plans: All work must adhere to the Structural Engineer's plans and calculations, which take precedence over any conflicting documents.  
Soils Report: Obtain and follow project soils report recommendations regarding foundation type, cement, and water-to-cement ratios.  
Concrete Installation: Provide labor, materials, and equipment for the installation of footings, slabs, and embedded members. Misplacement of members requires removal and replacement at Tradecontractor's expense.  
Coordination: Fully cooperate with other Tradecontractors (plumbing, electrical, framing) for timely completion and proper embed locations.  
Excavation: Garage slabs to be excavated and scored with at least four (4) squares.  
Footing Conditions: All footings must rest on undisturbed natural soil or approved compacted fill, with debris removed before concrete pour.  
Hauling: One-time haul of dirt spoils to be managed by Tradecontractor as per Project Superintendent's direction, ensuring positive drainage away from foundations.  
Reinforcement: Strict adherence to layout for reinforcing steel, bolts, and other embeds shown in structural drawings, with coordination required with the framing contractor.  
Fasteners: Use ASTM A153 compliant fasteners for treated wood.  
Backfill: Backfill plumbing and electrical trenches within the foundation perimeter.  
Garage Floor: Must slope a minimum of 1% towards the driveway; include a ¾” depression for the garage door.  
Moisture Control: Condition pad in accordance with the Soils Report before placement of visqueen and concrete.  
Concrete Mix: Follow concrete mix design per the Soils Report; approval required from Builder prior to use.  
Transit Time: Minimum 15 minutes and maximum 90 minutes for transit; trucks refusing at jobsite must dispose material offsite at their expense.  
Cleaning: Clean all exposed structural imbedded items after concrete placement, ensuring readiness for further installation.  
Washout Management: Construct, maintain, and remove all washouts within 48 hours of pours.  
Post Tensioning: Stress tendons after achieving 75% of design strength; install components as per post tension drawings.
207:
Concrete Foundations - Concrete Foundation SOW

Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install concrete foundations, including footings, garage slabs, and all associated hardware as per project requirements.

Plans Adherence: Plans and calculations by Structural Engineer take precedence over any conflicting documents.  
Soils Report: Contractor to follow project soils report, including foundation type and water-to-cement ratios.  
Concrete Installation: Furnish, place, tool, and finish footings, veneer footings, and embedded members. Misplaced members to be replaced at contractor's expense.  
Scheduling: Provide all necessary labor, materials, and equipment to meet published start and completion dates.  
Jobsite Coordination: Cooperate with other trades such as plumbing and electrical for the timely completion of work.  
Foundation Construction: Ensure foundations are built square, level, and plumb; layout building corners with offsets by others.  
Excavation: Include excavation of garage slabs, marked with a minimum of four scored squares.  
Footing Conditions: Footings to rest on firm natural soil or compacted fill; all loose dirt must be removed before concrete pouring.  
Dirt Spoils: One-time haul of dirt spoils to a designated on-site location, maintaining positive drainage flow away from foundations.  
Reinforcement Layout: Strictly follow layout for rebar, foundation bolts, and other hardware per structural drawings; coordinate closely with framing contractor.  
Fastener Specifications: Use plain standard finish fasteners in compliance with ASTM A153 for treated wood.  
Backfill Requirements: Backfill plumbing and electrical trenches within foundation perimeter.  
Excess Concrete: Recycle excess concrete off-site.  
Garage Floor Slope: Ensure garage floors slope a minimum of 1% towards the driveway.  
Sleepers: Install pressure-treated sleepers at all exterior openings.  
Grounding: Supply and install rebar ground rods for electrical panels, using 2pc UFER #4 rebar.  
Concrete Mix: Follow mix design as per soils report and structural drawings; not to be used without prior written approval.  
Transit Time: Minimum 15 minutes and maximum 90 minutes for concrete transit time; loads not meeting specs will be rejected.  
Curing: Apply curing as necessary.  
Flatwork Patching: Chip and patch all areas where flatwork meets foundations.  
Post-Tensioning: Stress post-tension tendons after slab achieves 75% design strength before loading.
174:
Concrete Foundations - Scope of Work  
Contractor shall furnish, place, and finish concrete foundations as per structural plans and soils report.  
Plans and Calculations: Adhere to Structural Engineer's plans; they supersede conflicting proposals.  
Soils Report: Obtain and follow recommendations for foundation type, cement type, and water-to-cement ratio.  
Concrete Work: Furnish, place, tool, and finish footings, slabs, and embedded members; misplaced members shall be replaced at contractor's expense.  
Coordination: Fully coordinate with plumbing, electrical, and framing contractors for timely completion and accurate placements.  
Foundation Requirements: All footings must rest on firm, undisturbed soil or approved compacted fill; trash must be removed before pouring.  
Backfill: Backfill plumbing and electrical trenches within foundation perimeter.  
Concrete Specifications: Concrete mix design in accordance with the Soils Report; maximum transit time is 90 minutes.  
Curing: Applied curing necessary as per requirements.  
Post Tensioning: Tendons must be stressed post achieving 75% design strength; paint cable ends with rust preventative paint prior to patching.  
Bollards: Supply and install pipe bollards as required by plans.  
Ground Rod: Supply and install the rebar ground rod; 2 pc UFER #4 rebar required.  
Garage Floors: Must slope a minimum of 1% towards the driveway for drainage; includes ¾” depression for garage door.  
Anchorage: Supply and install pressure treated sleepers; furnish washers and nuts for anchor bolts.  
Cleanup: Maintain and remove all washouts and waste within 48 hours after pours.  
Insurance and Cost: Any expenses from interference or removal of concrete due to negligence shall be borne by the contractor.
198:
Concrete Light Weight Topping SOW  
Scope of Work (Trade Specific Conditions)  
Contractor shall furnish and install a complete Gyp-Crete gypsum cement system per the plans and specifications.  
Labor, Tools & Equipment: Contractor to provide all labor, material, tools, equipment, and supervision for installation.  
Sound Reduction Mat: Install ¼” sound reduction mat as per manufacturer's recommendations.  
Testing Standard: Compressive strength test shall meet ASTM C472M.  
Material Handling: All materials must be delivered in unopened packages and protected from elements.  
Asbestos-Free Materials: Use asbestos-free materials tested per appropriate ASTM standards.  
Submittals: Provide manufacturer’s specifications, installation instructions, and certification of acoustic properties compliance.  
Preparation: Install moisture barrier and prime substrate per manufacturer's recommendations.  
Installation Responsibility: Contractor is responsible for material condition and installation of expansion joints through underlayment.  
Thickness: Gypsum cement underlayment thickness to be as noted on Exhibit D.  
Surface Requirements: Screed gypsum cement to a smooth surface with maximum 1/8 inch variation per 10 feet.  
Debris Removal: Excess material and debris shall be removed promptly during and after completion of work.  
Project Specifications Date: 07/01/2026.  
Refer to Exhibit C for additional requirements.
